description: An access-master record for a single namespaced permission (resource.action). Permissions are granted from four sources (Role, direct user grant, Designation, BusinessUnit) and deduplicated into a user's effective set; a special super-permission `all` (isAdminOnly) short-circuits every guard to allow everything. Each permission maps one-to-one to a row of the role-permission matrix. The shape is {value, description, isAdminOnly}.

fields.name: value
fields.label: Permission Value
fields.validation: format resource.action e.g. requisition.create, approval.approve, po.issue, suppliers.approve, payments.schedules.approve, routingRules.update, fieldConfig.manage, audit.view, all; natural primary key; unique; immutable
fields.purpose: The namespaced key the guard chain checks a user's effective set against (authenticate -> resolve permissions -> check required permission); the natural key roles/users/designations/verticals reference to grant access. The `all` value is the super-admin short-circuit.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.validation: non-blank; what the permission allows, e.g. 'Create a requisition'
fields.purpose: Explains in plain words what the permission grants so an admin granting it on the RBAC screen understands the action it unlocks; the label shown beside the value in the permission picker.

fields.name: isAdminOnly
fields.label: Admin Only
fields.validation: true for the super-permission `all` (and other admin-reserved grants); admin-only permissions are grantable only by a holder of `all`
fields.purpose: Marks a permission an ordinary admin screen cannot hand out; `all` is isAdminOnly so the super-admin grant cannot be assigned by anyone but an existing super-admin, preventing privilege escalation.

fields.name: resource
fields.label: Resource
fields.validation: the resource segment before the dot (e.g. requisition, approval, po, payments); derived from value; null for `all`
fields.purpose: The resource the permission acts on, derived from value, so the RBAC screen can group permissions by domain (Requisition, Approval, Payments) matching the role-permission matrix layout.

fields.name: action
fields.label: Action
fields.validation: the action segment after the dot (e.g. create, approve, issue); derived from value; null for `all`
fields.purpose: The action the permission allows, derived from value, completing the resource.action pair so the guard and the RBAC screen can reason about both halves of the namespaced permission.

invariants: A13 audit + SSE on every committed change; relaxing an SoD rule is recorded in the audit log

effective permissions = deduplicated union of role + direct user grant + designation + business-unit, plus the `all` super-permission short-circuit; no priority ordering; applied next request (no caching)
value is the natural key; `all` is isAdminOnly
every action in the permission namespace maps to one matrix row
